Researching the overall industry, your business competitor, and every detail you can find is very, very critical. This is something that should happen. Moment one, the second you get a great idea and you just write it down on an app game or you're at dinner or you're in the shower and you just come up with this great idea, the absolute first thing you should be doing is googling that market segment, finding out what competitors there are, finding out what retailers you need to get into, or who can build the app for you, or who can make this website for you, or what is the competition doing, what conventions are there, what industry locations, what industry events are there, is it something on meetup.com that you can go to an event, is it something on Eventbrite, do you have to travel to Vegas or New York or Paris, you just have to find out everything you can about your industry, what patents are there out there, what trademarks are out there, how much it costs to set up, how much it's going to take to create, how much does it cost, it's actually, if you're going to make massage chairs, it sounds fun and I'm going to make these great massage chairs to change the world and make better massage chair, but that could cost you hundreds of thousands, if not millions of dollars to create, and you didn't know that before you get super excited on your idea. And vice versa, you may say, oh, I want to start a new water bottle company or a new energy drink company. There's so many factors that go into something as simple as an energy drink, so I'll give you an example. There's 900 energy drinks on the market and you have this great idea of a great tasting flavored energy drink with this really catchy name. However, there's so many things you should be googling to finding out about this extremely competitive space. There's 900 beverages and only the top 10 are doing the billions and billions of dollars energy drink space. You need to find out the canning company. When I wanted to do my energy drink, I wanted to create a great tasting formula, so I started researching who's going to be my formulaist and who's going to be my scientist to make me this great flavor. I started researching all the flavor companies and I went and found 40 different flavors and I got lucky and got the cranberry pineapple and then a green tea. I thought about, wait, what am I going to do to stand out? They all taste like cough syrup, so I want to make mine taste better, so I lowered some of the flavoring to remove that thick cough syrup taste from it. Then I wanted to make it zero sugar, zero carbs, zero calories. How am I going to do that? I then researched and found out that I had to go through 7up. 7up was going to be my bottler. Then ball manufacturing was going to be the one to make my cans. I had to make sure that ball worked with 7up and I could get those together. I never would have known any of that just at the beginning thinking I want to make an energy drink. Whatever your idea is, do the research, talk to people in the space, go to any events you can, look at the forums, look at the blogs. I promise you there's an industry blog about everything. There's industry blogs and conventions about caskets, about funeral homes, about plumbers. Anything you can think of, I promise you there's over 400,000 conventions just on the West Coast. There's so many small events and so many mid-sized and large scale events in your industry space. Research it, find them, go to them and gather as much information as you can to figure out if you love it. How much is it going to take to make and how much can you make and how involved you want to get into that space.
